# getascraper/how-to-scrape-goodmoves

> goodmoves-jobs-scraper is an Apify Actor that scrapes Scottish charity and voluntary sector job listings from Goodmoves.org. It returns structured JSON-LD JobPosting output with apply metadata and optional OSCR charity register enrichment.
> Built with TypeScript, Cheerio, got-scraping. Free tier available.

## Overview

- Goodmoves.org jobs scraper and data extraction
- Extracts: title, company name, salary (min/max/period), location (street/city/region/postcode), posted/closing dates, employment type, industry, occupational category, workplace type, work hours, apply email, apply URL, apply PDF, description, OSCR charity number, organisation website
- Output formats: JSON, CSV, Excel, RSS, HTML
- Free tier: included (pay only for completed runs)

## Key Pages

- README: https://github.com/getascraper/how-to-scrape-goodmoves/blob/main/README.md
- Apify Store: https://apify.com/devanshlive/goodmoves-jobs-scraper
- Apify Console: https://console.apify.com/actors/devanshlive~goodmoves-jobs-scraper
- Source Actor: https://github.com/getascraper/actorstack/tree/main/actors/goodmoves-jobs-scraper

## How it works

1. Resolve target URL or fetch sitemap.xml from goodmoves.org
2. Enumerate all live job listing URLs (currently ~300 vacancies)
3. Fetch each vacancy page HTML
4. Parse Schema.org JobPosting JSON-LD block for structured data
5. Extract apply metadata (mailto link, apply URL, or PDF) from HTML
6. Optionally fetch organisation profile pages for website + OSCR charity number enrichment
7. Push one cleaned, normalised row per job to the Apify dataset

## Discovery modes

- `sitemap` (default) - Fetch all live vacancies from goodmoves.org/sitemap.xml
- `listing` - Pass start URLs as listing pages (e.g. /jobs-in/glasgow) and extract vacancy links from the page
- `none` - Pass start URLs as direct vacancy URLs

## API Usage

- Node.js SDK: https://docs.apify.com/api/client/js/
- REST API: https://docs.apify.com/api/v2
- MCP server: https://mcp.apify.com/

## Supported target URLs

- https://goodmoves.org/jobs-in/{location} (location listing)
- https://goodmoves.org/search?keywords={query} (search results)
- https://goodmoves.org/browse/{sector} (browse facets)
- https://goodmoves.org/vacancy/{salesforce-id}/{slug} (direct vacancy)

## Pricing

- Free tier: included
- Paid: $1.99 per 1,000 scraped jobs
- Competitor pricing: $2.60 to $2.90 per 1,000 (we are 24-31 percent cheaper)

## Output schema (highlights)

- type: "job"
- jobId: 15-char Salesforce ID (lowercase)
- jobId18Char: 18-char mixed-case Salesforce ID
- jobUrl: direct vacancy URL
- title: job title
- companyName: organisation name
- companyWebsite: organisation website (enrichment)
- oscrCharityNumber: Scottish Charity Register number (enrichment)
- location: { streetAddress, locality, region, country, postalCode, text }
- salary: { rawText, min, max, currency, period }
- applyType: "email" | "url" | "pdf" | "none"
- applyEmail: recruiter email (mailto)
- applyUrl / externalApplyUrl: application URL
- postedDate, closingDate: ISO 8601
- daysUntilClosing: computed
- employmentType, occupationalCategory, industry, workplaceType, workHours
- enrichment: { requested, organisationFound, errorMessage }
- scrapedAt: ISO 8601

## Use cases

- Track UK charity sector hiring trends
- Build salary benchmarks by cause, region, contract type
- Join job data to OSCR Scottish Charity Register
- Build RAG pipelines over Scottish nonprofit job descriptions
- Source-of-truth feed for recruitment agencies
- Longitudinal workforce studies on the voluntary sector

## Support

- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/getascraper/how-to-scrape-goodmoves/issues
- Apify Console: https://console.apify.com/actors/devanshlive~goodmoves-jobs-scraper/issues

## Related actors

- maximedupre/goodmoves-jobs-scraper - Competitor at $2.60/1k
- memo23/goodmoves-scraper - Competitor at $2.90/1k
- memo23/charityjob-scraper - UK-wide CharityJob board scraper
- memo23/s1jobs-scraper - Scotland all-sectors scraper
- memo23/highlandjobs-scraper - Scotland Highlands scraper
